skill.name: git-commit
skill.description: Creates Conventional Commit messages by inspecting Git status, staged changes, unstaged changes, and recent commit style.
skill.summary: Helps review local Git changes, stage relevant files, and create a clear Conventional Commit.
skill.author: 21pounder

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are true positives because the skill instructs the agent to run Git commands and create commits. The weak cryptography and environment file findings are false positives: the cited lines describe commit metadata or warn against committing secrets, and no malicious intent or prompt injection was found.

medium_findings.title: Repository Mutating Git Commands
medium_findings.description: TRUE POSITIVE: The skill directs the agent to inspect Git state, stage relevant files, execute a commit, and verify success. This is legitimate for a Git commit skill, but it can modify repository history and include unintended files if the agent or user chooses the wrong scope.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines clearly instruct running Git commands and creating commits. The purpose is legitimate, but repository mutation is a real operational risk.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Environment File Access
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The cited line tells the user not to commit sensitive files such as environment files or credentials. No evidence found that the skill reads, opens, exfiltrates, or requests environment secrets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The text is a protective guideline, not an instruction to access sensitive files. There is no network behavior or secret-handling workflow in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alerts
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The cited lines contain skill metadata, parameter descriptions, and Conventional Commit guidance. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic algorithm use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines do not mention cryptographic functions or algorithms. The alerts appear to be keyword matches on commit-related text.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Git Commands Through Bash
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash usage and instructs the agent to run Git status and diff commands before creating a commit. This pattern is expected for the workflow, but users should review staged files before committing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly allows Bash and lists Git commands as part of the methodology. The commands are scoped to Git operations and match the skill purpose.

content.value_statement: Manual commit preparation can miss changed files, staged diffs, or project message style. This skill reviews Git state and helps create a clear Conventional Commit.
actual_capabilities: Checks current repository status before committing.
Reviews staged and unstaged diffs to understand local changes.
Suggests a Conventional Commit type, scope, and subject.
Stages relevant files when needed for the requested commit.
Creates the Git commit and reports the resulting commit hash.
Warns against committing credentials or environment files.
limitations: Requires a local Git repository with command-line Git available.
Can only evaluate changes visible in the current workspace.
May need user confirmation when file selection is ambiguous.
Does not push commits to a remote repository.

use_cases.title: Commit a completed fix
use_cases.description: Review the working tree, choose a fix commit type, and commit only the relevant changed files.

use_cases.title: Standardize team commit messages
use_cases.description: Match the repository style and produce Conventional Commit messages for routine development work.

use_cases.title: Prepare clean review history
use_cases.description: Separate unrelated local changes and create a focused commit before opening a pull request.

prompt_templates.title: Commit current changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Commit my current changes with a clear Conventional Commit message.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a simple commit.

prompt_templates.title: Commit a specific type
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a fix commit for the staged changes after checking the diff.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the user knows the intended commit category.

prompt_templates.title: Commit selected work
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my unstaged changes, stage only the files related to the API update, and create a commit.
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the workspace contains unrelated changes.

prompt_templates.title: Match repository style
prompt_templates.prompt: Inspect recent commit messages, then commit the relevant changes using the same style and an appropriate scope.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for repositories with established commit conventions.

output_examples.output: Reviewed the working tree, staged the relevant files, and created a Conventional Commit for the implementation work.

output_examples.input: Create a fix commit for the staged diff.
output_examples.output: Confirmed the staged changes match a bug fix and created a focused fix commit with a concise subject.
Separated the documentation files from other local changes and created a documentation commit.

best_practices: Review the staged file list before allowing the commit.
Keep each commit focused on one logical change.
Check for credentials, environment files, and generated artifacts before committing.
anti_patterns: Do not commit unrelated changes together for convenience.
Do not rely on auto-generated messages without reviewing the diff.
Do not amend or force-change history unless the user explicitly asks.

faq.question: Does this skill push commits to a remote repository?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on local staging, commit creation, and reporting the result.

faq.question: Can it create Conventional Commit messages?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses commit types such as feat, fix, docs, refactor, test, and chore.

faq.question: Will it commit sensitive files?
faq.answer: The skill instructs the agent not to commit environment files, credentials, or other sensitive files.

faq.question: Can it work with unstaged changes?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect unstaged changes and stage relevant files when appropriate.

faq.question: Does it need Git installed?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow depends on local Git commands in the active repository.

faq.question: Can I provide my own commit message?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill supports an optional message override when the user provides one.
